findings.hypothesis_text: High cytotoxic T-cell infiltration predicts better overall survival in LUAD
findings.effect: HR 0.58 (95% CI 0.41-0.82), p=0.002, univariate; median OS 62 vs 38 mo
findings.author: Aisha Rahman
findings.markdown:
## Cytotoxic T-cell infiltration and OS in LUAD

High expression of a cytotoxic signature (CD8A, GZMB, PRF1, GZMK) stratified TCGA LUAD patients into significantly different overall-survival groups (median OS 62 vs 38 months, HR 0.58, 95% CI 0.41-0.82, p=0.002, univariate).

resources.citation: Gentles AJ et al. The prognostic landscape of genes across the cancer genome atlas. Nat Genet. 2015;47(11):1261-1268.

findings.hypothesis_text: Cytotoxic signature is a stage-independent prognostic marker in LUAD
findings.effect: HR approximately 0.79, p=0.11 after stage adjustment
findings.reason: Effect collapsed after stage adjustment - signal largely tracked stage. Do not re-run without a multivariate, stage-adjusted design.
findings.author: Aisha Rahman
findings.markdown:
## Stage-adjusted analysis (abandoned)

After adjusting for AJCC stage, the cytotoxic signature lost prognostic value (HR ~0.79, p=0.11). The univariate signal appears to track disease stage rather than adding independent information. **Do not re-run without a multivariate, stage-adjusted design.**
